events.name: Algowar (Code War)
events.description:
The Code Battle contest consists of three rounds: MCQ, Coding Challenge, and Rapid Fire Coding, designed to test participants’ programming knowledge and problem-solving abilities.

The first round is an MCQ quiz containing questions from Java and Python programming, data structures, and basic algorithms, covering fundamental programming concepts, syntax, and output analysis.

The second round is a coding challenge with problems similar to those on the LeetCode platform, focusing on array and string-based problems with varying difficulty levels.

The final round is a rapid fire coding round where participants must quickly solve simple programming problems within a limited time. This round evaluates speed, accuracy, and logical thinking in coding
rules: Languages allowed: C, C++, Java, Python
No internet access during the contest

events.name: Web development Event
events.description:
Round 1 : MCQ :Round 1 will contain 25  quiz questions on web development and tools and frame work for web design .

Round 2 : DEVELOPMENT Round 2 will be actual web design and development. (For example -  login page , or to-do list ) The best design will be shortlisted 

Optional : ( round -3 ) If there are more participants then round 3 will be conducted. The round 3 will include both design and working web page or a task . (For example: BMI Calculator , to - do list ).
rules: Don't use ai for script or editing

events.description: Present your research findings or innovative ideas on emerging technology topics. Showcase your knowledge and public speaking skills before expert judges.
rules: Topics: AI/ML, IoT, Blockchain, Cloud Computing, Cybersecurity
Abstract submission required before event day

events.description: Exhibit your innovative project with a working prototype or demo. Impress the judges with your creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.

Project report must be submitted beforehand

events.name: Connection
events.description: Connection Game is a fun non-technical event that tests participants’ thinking ability, observation skills, and teamwork. Teams must find the common connection between given words, pictures, or clues within the given time. The event consists of three rounds with increasing difficulty.

events.name: Brand It Now!!
events.description: Brand It Now is a fun and fast-paced event where participants create creative advertisements for the given product on the spot. Using imagination, humor, and confidence, contestants turn simple or unusual products into entertaining and convincing ads. Get ready for quick thinking, creativity, and lots of fun!
rules: Participants can take part individually or in teams of 2 to 4 members.
Basic props will be provided if required for the performance.
Preparation time will be provided before the presentation.
Participants must present their advertisement creatively and professionally.
The judges’ decision will be final and binding

events.name: Promptify X
events.description:
About Prompt Engineering:

Prompt engineering is the process of creating clear and effective instructions for AI to get accurate and useful results.Participants will be given a simple reference image and must recreate it by writing an effective AI prompt. This round tests their ability to observe visual details and convert them into clear instructions for AI generation.Rounds in the Event.

 Round 1:  First 5 mins refer the basics of prompt Engineering.Then 10 mins Q/A 10 questions.

 Round 2: Advance in promoting.See the picture and write the prompt for it,then the prompt is exact to that picture i.e the image contains all the elements as same in that picture
rules: 2 rounds: Basics, Creative Prompting, and Prompt Battle
Use of external tools is prohibited

events.name: code X paradise
events.description:
This is Code arena:

Round-1: Reverse Coding – Event Description: 

 Reverse Coding is a fun and challenging programming event where participants must identify the logic behind a given output. Instead of writing code from a problem statement, participants will be provided with the expected output, and they must write the correct program to produce that output. This round tests the participant’s logical thinking, coding skills, and problem-solving ability. The participant who successfully writes the correct code within the given time will score the highest.

Round-2 : HackerRank Contest – Event Description: 

 The HackerRank Contest is a competitive coding event where participants solve programming challenges within a limited time using the HackerRank platform. Participants must write efficient code to solve various problems related to algorithms, logic, and data structures. The contest evaluates coding accuracy, problem-solving skills, and time management. Participants are ranked based on the number of problems solved and the time taken to complete them.

events.name: Rhythm Rush
events.description:
~Start music game description : 

Start Music is a fun game where players identify Tamil cinema songs using small clues. The clues may be a BGM (background music), a line of lyrics, or a picture related to the song. Players listen or look at the clue and try to guess the correct song as quickly as possible.

The player who correctly identifies the song first earns points. This game tests players’ memory, listening skills, and knowledge of Tamil movie songs, making it exciting and entertaining for music lovers. 

•Round 1 – BGM Round:Players listen to a short background music (BGM) clip from a Tamil movie song and guess the correct song.

•Round 2 – Picture Round:Players see a picture related to a song (such as a movie scene, actor, or object) and identify the song connected to it.

•Round 3 – Missing Lyrics Round:Players are given a line from a song with missing words and must fill in the correct lyrics
rules: 3 rounds: Melody, Rapid Fire, and Finals
No use of phones or Shazam-like apps

events.name: IPL Auction
events.description:
This IPL Acution:

The event consists of three exciting rounds based on the Indian Premier League (IPL) to test participants’ cricket knowledge, observation skills, and strategic thinking.

Round 1: IPL Quiz Participants answer questions related to IPL teams, players, records, and history. This round tests the cricket knowledge and quick thinking of the teams.

Round 2: Historical moments of IPL Teams identify IPL players, team logos, stadiums, or memorable moments from pictures shown on the screen. This round checks visual recognition and cricket awareness.

Round 3: IPL Auction Teams act as IPL franchise owners and use a virtual budget to bid for players and build their team. The winner is decided based on team balance, player selection, and budget management.Overall, the event is designed to be fun, interactive, and competitive, encouraging teamwork while celebrating the excitement of the Indian Premier League
rules: Each team gets a virtual budget of ₹80 Crores
No communication between teams during bidding

events.name: Lab lockdown
events.description: The Lab Lockdown event consists of three exciting rounds focused entirely on electronics. In the first round, participants will take a mini digital electronics test to check their basic knowledge. The second round involves a lab circuit finding challenge, where participants must identify and analyze circuits in the lab. In the final round, teams will design and build a mini electronic circuit project, testing their creativity and practical skills.
rules: Participants must follow lab safety rules
Electronic components will be provided by the organizers
Mobile phones are not allowed during the event
Participants must complete the tasks within the given time
Any form of malpractice will lead to disqualification

events.name: Line following Robot
events.description: The Line Follower Robot Competition challenges participants to design and program an autonomous robot that can follow a predefined line track using sensors and a microcontroller. Each team will consist of 1-3 members. The robot must complete the track accurately and in the shortest possible time without human control. This event encourages skills in robotics, programming, electronics, and teamwork.
rules: Participants must bring their own robot and components
Robot must be completely autonomous (no human control)
Robot should follow the given line track correctly
Robot completing the track in the shortest time will score higher
Any damage to the track will lead to disqualification

This event encourages students to improve their technical knowledge, presentation skills, and creativity.

events.name: Chess & Carrom
events.description:
CHESS
Prove your tactical brilliance in this individual knockout tournament! The games strictly follow the official FIDE rules, including the touch-move rule. Compete in a fast-paced 10+5 minute time-controlled format where victory is claimed by checkmate, opponent resignation, or time out. Will you claim the board?

CARROM
Partner up for this thrilling black & white coin doubles tournament! Compete in a knockout format until one team successfully pockets all their coins along with the Queen. There are no time limits—only pure skill. Bring your own striker, report slightly before your match, and battle it out for exciting cash prizes!
rules: Rules: Games will follow official rules of FIDE
Touch-Move Rule: If a player touches a piece, it must be moved
Winning: Checkmate, opponent resignation, or time over
Decision: Referee/Organizers decision will be final
Winning Rule: Team that pockets all coins along with the Queen first wins
Striker: Players may use their own striker
Reporting: Teams must report 15 minutes before the match
Venue & Time: Will be announced in the WhatsApp group
Participation: Open to all branches, boys and girls
Prizes: Cash Prize for Winner & Runner-up | Participation Certificates for all

events.name: RAPID FIRE QUIZ
events.description: Test your speed and knowledge in this fast-paced quiz! Teams will face a series of rapid-fire questions to score points and prove their expertise.
rules: Participants can take part in teams (2 members).
Each team will be asked a series of rapid questions.
Time limit: 30 seconds – 1 minute per round.
Participants must answer quickly without using mobile phones or internet.
If a team cannot answer, the question may be passed to the next team.
The team with the highest score at the end of the quiz wins.
In case of a tie, a tie-breaker round will be conducted.

events.name: Find The Song
events.description: A song is a piece of music with words that are intended to be sung. It combines lyrics, rhythm, melody, and sometimes musical instruments to create a complete musical work. Guess the song based on the given clues!
rules: Points awarded for speed and accuracy

events.name: Mystery Bag Madness
events.description: Participants pick a bag containing a random object and must perform a funny act, pitch a product, or complete a creative task using it. The team with the most creative and entertaining performance wins.
rules: Acts must be creative and entertaining
Obscene or offensive content is strictly prohibited

events.name: Digital Design Arena (CAD)
events.description: Participants use CAD software to create 3D models, technical drawings, or innovative designs based on a given problem or theme. The most precise, creative, and well-presented designs win.

events.name: Auto Sleuths
events.description: Participants are given various automobile components like engine parts, suspension parts, or electrical components. They must identify each part correctly within a time limit. The fastest and most accurate participants win, testing their knowledge of automobile engineering systems.

events.name: TECHTALK ARENA (paper presentation)
events.description: Participants prepare and present a topic using slides, showcasing content clarity, creativity, and presentation skills. The best presentation with clear ideas and visuals wins.

events.name: INVENTRA (project Presentation)
events.description: Participants present projects related to automobile design, technology, or innovation, explaining the concept, working, and real-world application. The most practical, innovative, and well-explained project wins.

events.name: Fame Detective
events.description: Participants are given clues about a famous personality. Using these hints, they must identify the celebrity as quickly as possible. Fastest correct guesses earn points, and the team with the highest score wins.

events.name: Mystery Sketch
events.description: One person explains an object or scene, and the other team member draws it based on the explanation. The most accurate and creative drawings win.

events.name: TECHTALK ARENA (Paper Presentation)
events.description: Participants prepare and present an innovative topic related to civil engineering using presentation slides. The presentation will be evaluated based on clarity of content, creativity, technical knowledge, and presentation skills. The most informative and well-presented idea wins.
rules: Evaluated on clarity, creativity, technical knowledge, and presentation skills

events.name: INVENTRA (Project Presentation)
events.description: Participants showcase working models or innovative projects related to construction technology, structural design, sustainable materials, or infrastructure development. They must explain the concept, working principle, and real-world application. The most practical, innovative, and well-explained project wins.
rules: Working model or innovative project required
Must explain concept, working principle, and real-world application

events.name: SkyRise Challenge (Structure Design)
events.description: Participants must design and build a high-rise structure using ice cream sticks within the given time. The structure will be evaluated based on height, stability, creativity, and structural strength. The strongest and most efficient structure wins.
rules: Ice cream sticks will be used for building
Structure evaluated on height, stability, creativity, and structural strength
Must be completed within the given time

events.name: Draft Risk – CAD Modelling
events.description: Participants will be given a 2D building plan with specifications. Based on the given plan, participants must create the Plan, Elevation, and Section using AutoCAD. The drafting must be completed within a one-hour time limit. The designs will be judged on accuracy, creativity, detailing, and technical presentation. The most precise and well-presented drawing wins.
rules: A 2D plan with specifications will be provided
Participants must draft the Plan, Elevation, and Section using AutoCAD
Designs judged on accuracy, creativity, detailing, and technical presentation

events.name: Minute to Win
events.description: Participants must complete fun challenges within one minute. Each task tests speed, balance, and coordination. Challenges include: Cookie Crawl, Cup Tower Challenge, Emoji Acting Challenge, Water Bottle Flip, Rubber Band Shoot, Biscuit Tower, Chair Zigzag Race, and Straw Pyramid. Participants who complete the challenge successfully within the time limit win.
rules: Round 1 and round 2 will be conducted based on increasing  difficulty levels
Each challenge must be completed within one minute
Challenges test speed, balance, and coordination
Participants who complete the challenge within the time limit win

events.name: Mad Marketing
events.description: Participants are given random useless objects such as an empty bottle, ice cream sticks, or a piece of paper. They must creatively market the object as a useful or innovative product within a short time. The pitch will be judged based on creativity, confidence, humor, and marketing skills. The most convincing and entertaining marketing presentation wins.
rules: Round 1 and round 2 will be conducted based on increasing difficulty levels
Must creatively market the object as a useful product
Judged on creativity, confidence, humor, and marketing skills
